plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Reduce exposure in SMH and CRWD to lower concentration.
Significantly trim SPY to reduce single name concentration.
Buy AMD, a top-rated long setup, for new exposure.
Increase allocation to QQQI, the income ETF, for stability.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Trim SOXL to a small allocation and sell SPAL entirely.
Reduce concentration in SPY and CRWD to meet mandate limits.
Trim SMH to reduce semiconductor sector concentration.
Add new positions in AMD and SNOW, both strong long setups.
This blend aims for risk-first growth with a target of ~2.5% monthly.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% monthly return by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and new growth opportunities, managing risk as a priority.

moves.note: Add strong long setup, high score
Add strong long setup, high score

plan: Trim SPY and QQQI to free up capital and reduce concentration.
Trim SMH, a downtrending sector, to reallocate.
Add to SOXL, increasing leveraged ETF exposure.
Buy AMD, SNOW, and RDDT, focusing on highest-scoring long setups.
This aggressive strategy targets ~4% monthly but carries significantly higher drawdown risk due to leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for ~4% monthly return by concentrating in high-conviction long setups and increasing leveraged ETF exposure. It accepts substantially higher potential drawdowns and the risks of leverage decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and volatile single names (CRWD, SMH).
Trim SPY to reduce concentration.
Maximize allocation to QQQI, the primary income ETF.
Hold AMD as cash-like, not for growth.
This plan prioritizes stable monthly income and capital preservation, targeting ~1% monthly distributions.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by prioritizing income-generating ETFs like QQQI. It drastically cuts leverage and growth-oriented single names to minimize volatility and preserve capital.
